sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Republic Act No. 6713 — Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees. It upholds the time-honored principle that public office is a public trust. Enacted to promote high standards of ethics in public service.

sections.title: Key Definitions
rows: Government
National, local governments, all instrumentalities, agencies, branches, GOCCs, and subsidiaries
Public Officials
Elective and appointive officials and employees, permanent or temporary, career or non-career, including military and police — whether or not they receive compensation
Gift
A thing or right disposed of gratuitously in favor of another; includes simulated sales
Loan
Simple loan, commodatum, guarantees, financing arrangements intended to ensure approval
Substantial stockholder
Owns enough shares to elect a director of a corporation
Family of public officials
Spouses and unmarried children under 18 years of age
Conflict of interest
When a public official is a board member, officer, or substantial stockholder of a private corporation whose interests may conflict with faithful performance of official duty
Divestment
Transfer of title or disposal of interest in property by completely depriving oneself of right or title
Relatives
Persons related within the fourth civil degree of consanguinity or affinity, including bilas, inso, and balae

sections.title: Norms of Conduct (Section 4)
rows: Commitment to public interest
Always uphold public interest over personal interest; use government resources efficiently and honestly
Professionalism
Perform duties with excellence, professionalism, intelligence, and skill
Justness and sincerity
Remain true to the people; do not discriminate; respect the rights of others
Political neutrality
Provide service without unfair discrimination regardless of party affiliation or preference
Responsiveness to the public
Extend prompt, courteous, and adequate service; ensure openness of information; avoid red tape
Nationalism and patriotism
Be loyal to the Republic; promote locally produced goods; maintain Philippine sovereignty
Commitment to democracy
Commit to democratic values; maintain public accountability; uphold civilian authority
Simple living
Lead modest lives appropriate to position and income; no extravagant display of wealth

sections.title: Duties of Public Officials (Section 5)
rows: Act promptly on letters and requests
Respond within 15 working days from receipt; reply must contain action taken
Submit annual performance reports
Within 45 working days from year-end; must be open and available to the public
Process documents and papers expeditiously
Complete within a reasonable time; not more than 3 signatories
Act immediately on the public's personal transactions
Attend to anyone wanting services; act promptly and expeditiously
Make documents accessible to the public
All public documents accessible and readily available for inspection within reasonable working hours

sections.title: Prohibited Acts and Transactions (Section 7)
items: Financial or material interest in transactions requiring office approval
Owning/managing/accepting employment in private enterprises regulated by their office (without legal authority)
Engaging in private practice of profession without constitutional or legal authority
Recommending any person to any position in a private enterprise with official transactions
Using or divulging confidential/classified information for private interests
Soliciting or accepting any gift, gratuity, favor, entertainment, loan, or anything of monetary value in the course of official duties
Exception: Gifts of nominal value as souvenirs from foreign governments; scholarships, fellowships, or medical treatment; travel grants for travel entirely outside the Philippines.

sections.title: SALN — Statement of Assets, Liabilities, and Net Worth
items: All public officials must file under oath their SALN and Disclosure of Business Interests.
Filing deadlines: within 30 days after assumption of office; on or before April 30 of every year; within 30 days after separation from service.
Available to the public for 10 years after receipt.
Constitutional/national elective officials → Office of the Ombudsman
Senators and Congressmen → Secretaries of Senate/House
National executive officials → Office of the President
Regional/local officials → Deputy Ombudsman of their region
All other public officials → Civil Service Commission

sections.title: Divestment
items: Public official must resign from private business within 30 days of assumption of office.
Must divest shareholdings within 60 days of assumption.
